meta.title: Enterprise OS — Own Your Company's Intelligence as an Entity Graph
meta.description: You can outsource execution. What you must own is meaning, structure, relationships — your ontology. Enterprise OS is how you cultivate and govern your company's intelligence as an Entity Graph in the AI era.

hero.title: Enterprise OS
hero.subtitle: Why Your Enterprise AI Is Failing
hero.tagline: It's Not the AI You're Missing. Own Your Intelligence.
hero.author: Dave Tanaka / 田中訓
hero.disclaimer: This presentation reflects my personal views and does not represent the views of any employer or organization.

manifesto.headline: The Gold Rush Is Over
manifesto.subheadline: Don't Let the "Jeans Sellers" Win This Time.
manifesto.body1: In every gold rush, the ones who made the real fortune were those selling shovels and jeans, not the miners.
manifesto.body2: Internet, E-commerce, Marketing Automation... history repeats itself. Now, the AI "Big Wednesday" is here — a wave 30 meters high.
manifesto.body3: But if selling "AI prompts" or "training courses" has become the "essence" of AI — isn't that missing the point entirely?
manifesto.highlight: The most effective and correct way to use AI lies within your daily relationships with your customers, family, colleagues, and bosses.
manifesto.conclusion1: AI is not a magic wand. AI is not a teacher giving you the answers.
manifesto.conclusion2: AI is a partner that takes over the drudgery, creates time, and organizes your thoughts so you can connect more deeply with the people who matter.
manifesto.conclusion3: Your years of experience and judgment, honed in the field — that is the ultimate weapon when combined with AI.
manifesto.finalMessage: Our goal is to use AI to reclaim our human work and life.

problem.sectionTitle: The Problem No One Talks About
problem.quote: I once asked an internal assistant to summarize a document I had permission to access. It declined due to policy restrictions. That moment revealed something important: when AI appears "blocked," the issue is often not the model — it's the way knowledge is governed and structured.
problem.insight: The AI wasn't broken. It was underfed — missing the structure of meaning, the ontology, it needed.

stat1.label: of organizations now use AI in at least one function

stat2.label: Only a small share say AI is fully scaled enterprise-wide

stat3.label: Employees still spend a significant share of their time searching for information

rootCause.subtitle: It's not the AI. It's that meaning — entities and their relationships — is fragmented across systems, and no one owns the company's ontology.
rootCause.insight: Many disconnected systems • No shared map of meaning (ontology) • Fragmented search • AI can't connect meaning across them

postIt.sectionTitle: What Post-it® Notes Teach Us About Designing AI-Ready Organizations

step1.description: Spencer Silver invents a 'failed' adhesive. It sticks but peels off. Nobody sees a use for it.

step2.description: Art Fry needs a bookmark for his hymnal. He remembers Silver's 'failure.'

step3.description: Born from 15% Culture — the policy letting employees spend 15% of time exploring.

postIt.question: What if every organization were designed so employees naturally had space to explore — not as a policy, but as part of how the system works?

concept.sectionTitle: The Idea of an Enterprise OS
concept.subtitle: An Enterprise OS lets you own your company's intelligence as an Entity Graph — and cultivate and govern it over time.

layer1.title: Entity Graph (the meaning you own)
layer1.description: Your company's intelligence — what things mean and how they connect — owned as a graph of entities and relationships. This is what finally makes enterprise AI actually work.

layer2.title: Cultivate & Govern (GitHub as the box)
layer2.description: The graph is alive. You grow and version it in a box (GitHub). commit = how meaning changes, branch = regional/department variants, pull request = reviewed changes to meaning, fork = extend to partners. The box is replaceable; the graph inside is the point.

layer3.title: Human Flourishing
layer3.description: When AI handles the routine, time comes back — not for more work, but for human work: connecting more deeply with customers, partners, colleagues, and family.

proof.subtitle: Inspired by Anker's product conference, I built two Entity Graphs — "the conference itself" and "the mobile-battery market." Same method, different subjects. This is what owning the meaning — who it's for, how things connect — looks like, instead of specs or meeting minutes.

cards.title: Anker Power Conference 2026
cards.desc: Products, tech, speakers and booths turned into a walkable map of nodes and relationships. Owning the meaning of an event.

cards.title: Mobile Battery Market Entity Graph
cards.desc: 30 products × user segments × contexts × regulations. "Light up everything for business travelers" — one move in a graph. With a meaning lens, AND filters and guided tours.

proof.note: Both are live Entity Graphs that run entirely in the browser.

gitFoundation.sectionTitle: A Practical Foundation: Git as the Box that Cultivates Meaning
gitFoundation.subtitle: Software engineering has dealt with this problem for decades. The opportunity now is to apply it company-wide.

whatIsGit.title: What is Git?
whatIsGit.subtitle: Similar to "Track Changes" — but applied to all corporate knowledge.
points: Every change recorded: who, when, and why
Nothing is ever lost — full rollback capability
Teams work in parallel without conflicts
Built-in review & approval before changes go live

proposal.title: The Proposal
proposal.subtitle: Use Git as the box to cultivate and govern the meaning you own — not just code.
points: Beyond code → cultivate and govern the meaning you own
AI reads from Git → enterprise AI gains history, context, and traceability
Existing approval workflows integrate naturally
Decades of innovation, searchable & connected

stats.line3: Used across industries — from manufacturing to finance to healthcare

howItWorks.subtitle: Your owned Entity Graph at the center — GitHub is the box that cultivates and governs it, connecting data, AI, and outputs.

center.title: GitHub Enterprise
center.subtitle: The box for your Entity Graph

howItWorks.review: Review & Approval — integrated with Git Pull Request workflow

commit.description: Every change recorded with history and rationale

branch.description: Regional or departmental variants without breaking the source

faq.sectionTitle: Making It Real: Three Questions You're Probably Asking
faq.subtitle: Three questions you're probably asking — and honest answers

q1.question: How do existing systems connect?
q1.answer: Your systems stay exactly as they are. Enterprise OS just connects them. GitHub Actions syncs data through REST APIs. ERP, CRM, SharePoint — mature connectors already exist for all of them. No custom middleware required. Existing review tools integrate through webhooks and PR status checks.

q2.question: What about security?
q2.answer: Protected by the same infrastructure that secures the world's most critical software.
points: Enterprise Cloud with data residency options
Role-Based Access Control (RBAC)
Advanced Security (GHAS) with automatic secret scanning
SOC2 Type II / ISO 27001 / FedRAMP

q3.question: What does it look like for users?
q3.answer: Most people never see GitHub — they just experience work running better.
examples: Marketing → uploads through SharePoint as usual / Approvers → use existing review tools
Engineers → work directly in GitHub / Leadership → just ask the AI

everyoneBuilds.subtitle: GitHub + AI doesn't just support engineers — it lets everyone build.

before.title: Before: Engineers Build, Others Request
points: Marketing needs a page → files ticket → waits 3 weeks
Sales needs a report → asks IT → gets it next quarter
Regional team needs content → email chain → lost in translation

after.title: After: Everyone Builds, AI Assists
points: Marketing describes need → AI generates → PR review → live
Sales asks AI → gets answer with sources → acts now
Regional team forks → AI translates + adapts → approved → done

efficiency.quote: The companies that win in the AI era won't be the ones with the best AI. They'll be the ones that use AI to become more human.

gettingStarted.subtitle: You don't have to boil the ocean. Start with one workflow.

steps.title: Pick One Pain Point
steps.description: Find the workflow where knowledge fragmentation hurts the most. Content localization, customer support, regulatory compliance — choose the one people complain about every day.

steps.title: Build a PoC on GitHub
steps.description: Move that workflow's documents into a GitHub repository. Set up version control, branch for regional variants, and enable PR-based approval. Show that it works.

steps.title: Add the AI Layer
steps.description: Connect an AI assistant to read from the repository. Now the AI has context, history, and structure. Watch it go from "generic" to genuinely useful.

steps.title: Expand Organically
steps.description: Success drives adoption. Once one team sees results, others will ask to join. Each connected workflow makes the platform smarter.

foil.quote: This loop becomes the new IP of the firm.
foil.context: Even Microsoft's CEO now argues the edge lies in the learning loop, not the model — that you should be able to swap a 'generalist' model without losing the 'company veteran' expertise built into your system.
foil.counter: But he draws the line at owning the loop — the process — and value there ultimately routes back to Microsoft's platform. We draw it one level deeper: what you must own is the thing the loop runs on — your meaning, your ontology.

reasons.title: The AI Gap Is Widening
reasons.description: 88% of organizations use AI, but only 39% see measurable impact (McKinsey, Nov 2025). The difference isn't the AI — it's the foundation of meaning you own. Companies that own this first will pull ahead for good.

reasons.title: Knowledge Is Walking Out the Door
reasons.description: In the U.S., Baby Boomers are retiring at 10,000+ per day. Every departure without knowledge capture is permanent loss. Enterprise OS makes knowledge transfer part of daily workflows — not a side project.

reasons.title: The Technology Is Ready
reasons.description: Git, GitHub Enterprise, AI APIs — everything needed already exists. The missing piece isn't technology. It's the vision to connect what's already there.

ontologyOwnership.title: Owning Meaning: Ontology & Knowledge Graphs
items: Google, "Introducing the Knowledge Graph: things, not strings" (2012) — the official shift from strings to entities

dbt Labs / AtScale — the semantic layer: a single shared meaning for metrics across the company
